us-politics.headline: Trump Warns Iran as Rescue Mission Raises War Stakes
us-politics.body:
President Trump declared Iran could be "taken out in one night" at a press conference Sunday, hours after the U.S. completed a high-risk mission to recover a wounded airman shot down over Iranian territory. Trump called Iran's subsequent ceasefire proposal "significant" but "not good enough," keeping his deadline in place. Israel struck an Iranian petrochemical plant the same day, adding pressure.

A House Republican predicted troop deployment is likely, while a senior GOP defense voice argued the U.S. could reopen the Strait of Hormuz without a ground invasion. JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon warned a prolonged conflict could tip the economy into recession. Sen. Bernie Sanders called Trump's threats the "ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual." Analysts note Iran may not need to win outright - simply not losing could exhaust U.S. resolve, as past conflicts have shown.

sources.title: Live updates: Trump warns all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’ as he sticks to deadline
Trump says US will go after ‘leaker’ of info on downed pilot in Iran
Sanders on Trump Iran f-bomb threat: ‘Ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual’
Trump touts airman rescue mission, boasts Iran could be 'taken out in 1 night'
‘God is good’: Inside the high-risk US mission to save a wounded airman shot down in Iran
Iran could defeat the US just by not losing
House Republican predicts Trump will deploy troops in Iran: ‘I just don’t see any other way’
Trump: Iran ceasefire proposal ‘significant,’ ‘not good enough’
Top GOP defense voice breaks with invasion fears, says US can reopen Hormuz without ground war
Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
Trump reveals Iran made 'significant proposal' after ultimatum, but 'not good enough'
Israel hits petrochemical plant in Iran ahead of Trump deadline
Dimon warns Iran war could sink economy into a recession

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Sides With Bannon on Jan. 6 Conviction
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court ruled in favor of Steve Bannon Sunday, backing his bid to dismiss his January 6-related conviction. The decision marks a significant legal victory for one of Trump's closest allies and is likely to intensify debate over accountability for the Capitol riot.

Separately, Rep. Jamie Raskin demanded the Justice Department hand over details of a settlement reached with former National Security Adviser Michael Flynn. The Flynn inquiry adds to broader Democratic scrutiny of DOJ conduct under the Trump administration. Rep. Don Bacon, a Republican, also broke with his party's leadership, calling Defense Secretary Hegseth's firing of top military officers "not morally right nor wise."

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Raskin demands DOJ turn over details on settlement with Michael Flynn
Don Bacon on Hegseth firing top military officers: ‘Not morally right nor wise’

us-politics.headline: Trump Accounts Launch, BNY Named as Manager
us-politics.body:
The Treasury Department named BNY as the manager for Trump Accounts ahead of the savings program's formal launch. The accounts, a signature domestic policy initiative, are designed to give Americans a new federally managed investment vehicle. Details on eligibility and contribution limits remain limited.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman separately released a policy blueprint calling for federal taxation and regulation of artificial intelligence, signaling that major tech players are moving to shape the rules before Congress does. The NHTSA closed its investigation into Tesla's "Actually Smart Summon" feature, clearing the automaker of safety concerns that had drawn regulatory attention.

sources.title: BNY to manage Trump Accounts ahead of program’s launch
OpenAI’s Altman releases blueprint for taxing, regulating artificial intelligence
Regulator closes investigation into Tesla ‘actually smart summon’ feature

us-politics.headline: Late Show Era Ends; CBS Fills Colbert's Slot
us-politics.body:
CBS confirmed that Byron Allen's "Comics Unleashed" will take over the Late Show time slot following Stephen Colbert's departure. The move ends a decade-long run for Colbert and signals a broader shift away from traditional late-night political commentary at a major broadcast network.

The decision comes as Democrats face mounting internal pressure. Opinion writers across the political spectrum note the party lacks a coherent rebuilding strategy and cannot afford to rely on Trump's stumbles alone to recover ground. The cultural and political landscape is shifting simultaneously - and neither party has a clear answer for what comes next.

sources.title: CBS announces what’s taking over Colbert’s ‘Late Show’ time slot
Democrats have too many troubles to cheer Trump’s failures

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Far North Queensland Weeks After Narelle
au-politics.body:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is tracking toward Far North Queensland, threatening a region still recovering from Cyclone Narelle, which struck just three weeks ago. Authorities are monitoring the system closely as communities brace for a second major weather event in quick succession.

The back-to-back cyclones put emergency services and local infrastructure under renewed pressure. Residents in affected areas have been urged to prepare early and follow official guidance as the storm's path becomes clearer.

sources.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: Election Watch: Murdoch Son-in-Law Backs Farrer Independent Candidate
au-politics.body:
Rupert Murdoch's pro-climate son-in-law is funding an independent candidate in the New South Wales seat of Farrer, adding an unusual dimension to the federal election campaign. The move highlights growing financial backing for independents from donors outside traditional party networks.

The Spectator has separately flagged Pauline Hanson's One Nation as a rising force, arguing the party is gaining momentum heading into the election. The two stories together illustrate the pressure building on major parties from both the teal independent movement and the populist right.

sources.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent
Pauline Hanson: straight to number one

au-politics.headline: AI Risks Grow: Jobs, Environment Assessments and Robodebt Warnings
au-politics.body:
Scientists are warning that using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments could produce robodebt-style failures, where automated systems make consequential decisions without adequate human oversight. The concern centres on accountability gaps when errors occur at scale.

Separately, a broader examination of AI's impact on the workforce finds the technology is already reshaping employment across multiple sectors. The Spectator takes a more sceptical view of government digital initiatives, criticising what it calls a headache-inducing approach to online public services. Across the spectrum, the debate over who bears responsibility for AI-driven outcomes is sharpening.

sources.title: A world without work. How do we survive the AI revolution?
Using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments risks ‘robodebt-style’ failures, scientists say
Free online therapy from a headache-inducing government

au-politics.headline: Iran War Updates: Ceasefire Rejected, Chinese AI Aids Targeting
au-politics.body:
Iran has rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal from the United States as fighting continues, with Trump separately praising the rescue of a downed US airman. The rejection signals little near-term diplomatic progress despite ongoing pressure.

A significant intelligence concern has emerged alongside the conflict: reports indicate China is providing satellite data that allows Iranian forces to target US military assets with greater precision. The claim, if confirmed, would mark a serious escalation in indirect Chinese involvement in the conflict and adds a new layer to already strained US-China relations.

sources.title: Trump praises airman's rescue as Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal
Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'

world.headline: US-Israeli Strikes Intensify as Iran Deadline Looms
world.body:
US and Israeli forces have escalated strikes across Iran, hitting residential areas, a major university, and pharmaceutical facilities. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed the campaign is intensifying as Trump's deadline - tied to demands over the Strait of Hormuz - approaches. Trump called the deadline final while Iran pushed a counter-proposal to end the conflict.

Iranians are bracing for strikes on power plants and civilian bridges, with analysts warning of catastrophic infrastructure collapse. Bombing of pharmaceutical producers is already threatening patients dependent on locally made medicines. A US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran during the campaign, an operation Trump publicly celebrated at a White House Easter event.

Democrats condemned Trump's threat to commit what they called war crimes. Most Republicans backed the approach. North Korea, meanwhile, is keeping its distance from Tehran, according to South Korean intelligence.

sources.title: Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
Trump warns deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
Trump praised airman’s rescue in Iran during White House Easter event
'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives

world.headline: Gaza Violence Continues as WHO Worker Killed
world.body:
Israeli forces fired on a World Health Organization vehicle in southern Gaza, killing one person. Separately, ten people died in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and a rival militia. The violence continues to grind down civilian life across the territory.

A camp offering children psychological safety has drawn attention amid the relentless conflict. One grandmother described losing her entire family to Israeli attacks. The war shows no sign of pausing.

sources.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
Gaza camp offers children a sense of safety
Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report
Gaza grandmother loses her family due to Israeli attacks

world.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Conflicts Disrupt Supply
world.body:
The war in Iran is sending shockwaves through global energy markets, with Asia bearing the sharpest impact as oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain under threat. Libya's internal oil disputes are compounding the crisis, stoking fears across Europe over supply security. Cairo has begun dimming its streets as soaring energy prices bite.

Ukraine struck the Russian Black Sea energy hub at Novorossiysk, adding another pressure point to an already strained global market. The convergence of conflicts across the Middle East and Eastern Europe is pushing prices to levels that are reshaping daily life far beyond the war zones.

sources.title: How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar
Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears
Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk

world.headline: Artemis II Sets Record; Myanmar Junta Chief Becomes President
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew broke the record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth, marking a milestone in the push to return people to deep space.

In Myanmar, junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was elected president, consolidating military control after years of brutal crackdown following the 2021 coup. The move raises questions about any path back to civilian governance. Elsewhere, seven Eritrean footballers failed to return home after an international match, the latest in a pattern of players seeking asylum abroad.

sources.title: Artemis II breaks record for farthest human travel from Earth
Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate AI Data Centers in Abu Dhabi
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers in Abu Dhabi, raising security concerns over the Gulf region's growing role as an AI infrastructure hub. The threats come as the UAE has positioned itself as a key partner in the Stargate project, which represents hundreds of billions in planned AI investment.

The timing is awkward for OpenAI, which last week published a sweeping economic vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week to manage AI's disruption of labor markets. Building the physical infrastructure to power that future now faces geopolitical friction that no policy paper addresses.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week

ai.headline: AI Coding Tools Face Backlash Over Quality and Hype
ai.body:
Criticism of AI-assisted development is sharpening. A widely circulated essay argues that "vibe coding" - writing software by prompting AI without understanding the output - has become a dangerous cult, producing brittle code that developers cannot maintain or debug. The piece challenges the assumption that lowering the barrier to writing code is the same as lowering the barrier to building good software.

Separately, a growing thread on GitHub documents that Claude Code has become "unusable for complex engineering tasks" following Anthropic's February updates, with engineers reporting degraded reasoning and increased errors on multi-step problems. Freestyle, a new startup, is pitching sandboxed environments for AI coding agents as one structural fix - isolating agent actions before they touch production systems.

sources.title: The Cult of Vibe Coding Is Insane
Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with the Feb updates
Launch HN: Freestyle: Sandboxes for AI Coding Agents

ai.headline: Google Launches Offline AI Dictation; ChatGPT Adds App Integrations
ai.body:
Google quietly shipped an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, processing speech entirely on-device without sending audio to the cloud. It is a notable privacy-forward move from a company that built its business on cloud data, and signals growing confidence in running capable AI models locally on consumer hardware.

OpenAI moved in the opposite direction, deepening ChatGPT's connections to external services including DoorDash, Spotify, and Uber. The integrations let users trigger real-world actions through conversation. Together the two releases sketch out a split emerging in consumer AI - one path toward private, local processing, another toward AI as a universal remote for the internet.

sources.title: Google quietly releases an offline-first AI dictation app on iOS
How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others

ai.headline: Adobe's Hosts File Manipulation Draws Sharp User Backlash
ai.body:
Adobe has been caught silently modifying the system hosts file on users' machines to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed - a technique typically associated with malware, not enterprise software. The modification serves no user benefit and was made without disclosure, reigniting long-running frustration with Adobe's aggressive software practices.

The incident lands as a case study in eroding user trust. Developers and power users who discovered the change reacted with alarm, not because the technical impact is severe, but because the behavior treats the user's machine as Adobe's property. It is the kind of move that accelerates migration to alternatives.

sources.title: Adobe modifies hosts file to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $70K as Iran Tensions Drive Volatility
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed above $70,000 Monday before profit-taking pulled it back, with geopolitical noise doing most of the driving. Trump's ultimatum to Iran rattled markets early, but reports of ceasefire talks sparked a quick rally - illustrating how sensitive crypto has become to macro headlines.

Analysts see contrarian bottoming signals building, including weakening bearish momentum and thin short positioning. Whether that translates into a sustained move toward $75,000 depends heavily on how the Iran situation develops and whether broader risk appetite recovers. Prediction markets are now tracking Iran war probabilities in real time, functioning as a live macro sentiment gauge for traders.

sources.title: Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum

btc.headline: Strategy Buys More Bitcoin While Sitting on $14.5B Loss
btc.body:
Strategy added 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million last week, pushing its total holdings to nearly 767,000 BTC. The purchase came despite the firm reporting paper losses exceeding $14.5 billion in Q1 - a figure that underscores just how exposed the company is to price swings.

The buy signals continued conviction from Michael Saylor's firm, but it also raises questions about risk tolerance at that scale. Meanwhile, Bitmine moved its stock listing to the NYSE as its ether treasury hit 4.8 million ETH, suggesting the corporate crypto treasury model is spreading well beyond bitcoin.

sources.title: Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC

btc.headline: Dimon Warns on Tokenization Rivals as IMF Flags Systemic Risk
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual letter to warn that blockchain and stablecoins are producing real competitors - and that the bank needs to move faster. JPMorgan already runs Kinexys, its own tokenization platform, but Dimon's tone suggests urgency rather than comfort.

The IMF pushed back from the other direction, warning that tokenization could drag crypto's volatility and liquidity risks into mainstream financial markets. China's tax authority separately urged banks to adopt blockchain for lending. The convergence of Wall Street ambition, regulatory caution, and state-level adoption is reshaping how seriously institutions treat the technology - even as they disagree sharply on the risks.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
China's tax authority urges banks to implement blockchain for lending services
IMF warns tokenization could bring crypto risks into global financial markets

btc.headline: US Crypto Legislation Advances as Prediction Markets Win in Court
btc.body:
A Senate Banking Committee member confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation, adding momentum to what has been a slow-moving regulatory process. Clear rules on asset classification remain the central sticking point between lawmakers and the industry.

On the markets side, a federal appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports prediction markets, a significant legal win for the sector. Polymarket also launched a full exchange upgrade, taking direct control of its order book and oracle systems. Together, the moves suggest prediction markets are maturing fast - legally and technically - just as Washington starts to catch up.

sources.title: US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

food.name: Miso-Glazed Cabbage Steaks
food.description: Thick-cut cabbage slabs roasted until caramelised and lacquered with a sweet-salty miso butter glaze
food.tip: Press the cabbage slices dry before roasting and use the highest oven heat you have — the char on the edges is the whole point

exercise.name: The Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for timed sets, building grip strength, core stability, and posture after a weekend of likely sitting

learn.description: Stochastic resonance is the counterintuitive phenomenon where adding a precise amount of random noise to a weak signal actually makes that signal easier to detect — it has been observed in neurons, sensory systems, and even climate models
learn.why: It quietly dismantles the assumption that noise is always the enemy of clarity, and once you understand it you start noticing its fingerprints in biology, engineering, and perception science everywhere
